_comment: The four systems under comparison. The two systems in each pair (A-research/A-vanilla, B-research/B-vanilla) MUST be identical except for 'grounding' and what that implies. Within a pair, model, embedding_model, chunk_size, overlap, and top_k must match exactly so the only variable is research grounding. Fill A-research from the ACTUAL configuration of the already-built system before running anything. Do not invent values.

shared._generation_model_note: Confirmed by user 2026-06-06: A-research's code (query.py MODEL) generates with claude-sonnet-4-6. The 'Haiku API key' was the user's name for the key, not the model. All four systems use this same model so the only variable within a pair is research grounding.

pair_invariants._comment: These fields MUST be identical between the two systems within each pair. The runner should assert this before running and halt if violated.

systems._retrieval_note: FTS5 keyword + one-hop relationship graph walk + thread/flag/decision direct lookup, per ARCHITECTURE.md. Values below reflect the actual built system: query.py uses FTS5 (no embeddings), indexes whole-memo search_text (no fixed chunking), keyword_search LIMIT 8, context capped at MAX_CONTEXT_MEMOS=12.

Truly standard RAG. Flatten spreadsheet rows to text, fixed-size chunk, embed, top-k cosine, stuff into generic prompt. NO entity normalization, NO relationship graph, NO bounded vocab, NO thread/flag awareness, NO citation requirement, NO structured reasoning prompt. Do not improve it.
systems._embedding_note: Confirmed by user 2026-06-06: local off-the-shelf sentence-transformers model (no API key, runs offline). A-vanilla and B-vanilla use the SAME model. This is the exact model string loaded at run time.

systems._retrieval_note: Run full ARCHITECTURE.md extraction on raw PDFs (normalization, coreference, sequential bounded-vocab extraction, memo-level chunking) building a structured knowledge base, then retrieve and reason over that.
systems._chunk_note: Per ARCHITECTURE.md: never chunk below a full memo section. Memo-level chunking for relation extraction.

systems._retrieval_note: Chunk raw PDF text, embed, top-k cosine, stuff into generic prompt. NO extraction step at all. The LLM sees raw chunks of memo text, not structured fields. Do not improve it.
systems._embedding_note: Must match A-vanilla's embedding model exactly. Confirmed local sentence-transformers, same string as A-vanilla.

experiment_B_ingestion._comment: Both B systems consume the SAME resolved document set. Resolve once, confirm, then both ingest the byte-identical set.
experiment_B_ingestion.page_note: Plain HTTP fetch returns only nav chrome. Render with headless browser OR find the document-listing endpoint. Memos served via showpublisheddocument/{id}/... URLs, not static anchors.

filters.date_ceiling_note: Corrected from 2026-04-16 by user 2026-06-07. The premise that the spreadsheet ends 2026-04-16 was factually wrong: the spreadsheet's own date_published runs through 2026-04-26 (8 memos APR-009..016 dated Apr 21-26). Using the Apr 16 ceiling would itself cause the A-vs-B window mismatch it was meant to prevent (A would cover through Apr 26, B only through Apr 16). The ceiling is set to the spreadsheet's TRUE last date so both experiments cover the identical window. Exclude every memo dated after this ceiling.
filters.restrict_note: Set true by user 2026-06-07. Within the year+ceiling window the website lists 79 memos; the spreadsheet's human curators included only 77 of them (they omitted 2 weekly-report memos, doc ids 95838 + 95840). Restricting B to the spreadsheet's exact 77 doc ids makes A and B cover the byte-identical curated document set, not just the same time window. The spreadsheet doc ids are read from reno.db memos.source_url. Memos in-window but not in the spreadsheet set are excluded with reason 'not_in_spreadsheet'.

experiment_B_ingestion.expected_count_note: Spreadsheet lists 77 memos through 2026-04-26 (its true end). With restrict_to_spreadsheet_docset=true the B set is exactly those 77 (overlap 77/77, zero divergence). The 2 in-window website extras (95838, 95840) are excluded as 'not_in_spreadsheet'.
